summary: The test suite comprehensively maps to every key requirement: main/master→latest, PR→pr-{number}, semver tags, feature branch sanitization, and workflow structure validation are all exercised. Rigor is strong — pre-release semver suffixes, nested slashes, underscore-to-hyphen conversion, SHA truncation edge cases, precedence rules (PR overrides branch-sha), and negative assertions (latest absent on PRs, etc.) are all present. Helper unit tests for Get-SanitizedTag and Get-ShortSha cover boundary inputs (SHA shorter than 7 chars). The main design concerns: (1) Pester v5 does not expose functions defined in BeforeAll to It blocks via normal lexical scope — Invoke-ActJob defined in BeforeAll will be invisible to the It blocks that call it, causing the entire Act Integration Tests describe to throw at runtime rather than fail gracefully; (2) $WorkflowContent and similar variables in Workflow Structure Tests share the same risk depending on Pester version; (3) the integration test layer is heavily environment-dependent (Docker daemon, act, network pull of runner images) with no skip/guard logic, making the suite non-deterministic in CI environments that lack those dependencies; (4) exact string matches against act output (e.g. 'PASS.*branch-sha', 'Job succeeded|success') are brittle against minor act version or output-format changes. Unit-level tests for the core logic are solid and would reliably catch regressions; the integration tier is structurally flawed enough to reduce confidence in its practical value.
